claim: Three tiers of AI coding tools: (Tier 1) AI Copilots with autocomplete, (Tier 2) AI Coding Assistants with multi-file context and advanced prompting, (Tier 3) AI Software Engineers end-to-end automation
actionable: Choose coding tools based on task: copilots for line-level completion, coding assistants for file-level refactoring, software engineers for full features (when stable)
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers

claim: AI coding assistants (Cursor, Aider, Continue) support selection prompts with context references (e.g., @filename) allowing multi-file reasoning that copilots cannot do
actionable: Use selection prompts with file references in coding assistants for cross-file consistency; copilots lack this context capability for related files
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers

claim: Terminal prompts in coding assistants enable command generation from documentation context; e.g., reference README then ask 'how do I do X' and get proper bash command
actionable: Use terminal prompts in coding assistants to generate CLI commands from documentation; reduces need to memorize tool-specific syntax
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers

claim: Codebase question-answer (Command+Enter in Cursor) scans entire codebase for context; enables 'how does this codebase work' questions without manual navigation
actionable: When jumping to new codebases, use codebase QA feature to understand architecture quickly; this accelerates onboarding and context switching
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers

claim: Web search in coding assistants enables prompts that reference live documentation; ask coding assistant to fetch docs and answer questions in single interaction
actionable: Use web search prompts in coding assistants for questions requiring current API documentation; this avoids training data cutoff issues
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers

claim: AI Software Engineers (Devon, Copilot Workspace) are too early for production use; unreliable, costly, and slow despite promise of end-to-end task automation
actionable: Avoid AI Software Engineers for production work currently; focus on Tier 2 coding assistants which are proven and reliable for real workflows
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers

claim: Most boilerplate, setup, and wrapper logic can be generated by AI coding assistants; the 5-10% of code requiring deep engineering thinking is where human focus should be
actionable: Use coding assistants to generate boilerplate and structure; focus your engineering effort on domain logic and complex reasoning where human insight adds value
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers

claim: Not using AI coding assistants means engineers will be 2-10x less productive than peers using them; adoption is now mandatory for competitive software development
actionable: Adopt AI coding assistants immediately; not using them puts you at significant productivity disadvantage relative to peers
source_title: AI Coding Tool Breakdown: AI Copilots vs AI Coding Assistants vs AI Software Engineers
